content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach consistently named visible reads at the moments they mattered, and grounded questions in those reads rather than asking blind. Turn 4 names the rush-from-feeling-to-list (the rush signature in operation). Turn 8 names a specific somatic/linguistic event ('you just stopped yourself mid-sentence') in the moment. Turn 10 names the said-vs-shown gap ('you named something real... and then you moved pretty fast to dismiss it'). Each read precedes the inquiry, and each read changes the direction of the conversation — the client lands in the body by turn 11. Reads are specific (pace, self-interruption, dismissal) rather than generic mood-labels. Coach avoided matching the list energy and refused to strategize.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 8, 10],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Content-level question about the feeling; no read named yet, but appropriately doesn't engage the list."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the rush signature explicitly: 'you moved pretty quickly from the feeling to the list.' Read precedes question."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Good false-end question but no read named."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the self-interruption in real time — 'you just stopped yourself mid-sentence' — a precise linguistic/somatic read at the exact moment it matters."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the said-vs-shown pattern: client voiced something real then dismissed it. Specific, timely, and pivots the conversation into feeling."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Tracks the apology/laugh-it-off move and redirects to felt experience."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Strong reframe question but no explicit read of the client's state at that moment."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Reads were specifically about pace, self-interruption, and dismissal — all core AoA reads. Coach did not name somatic signature (breath, body) explicitly, but the linguistic/energetic reads were operative and well-timed."
}
